reviews.text: I would give a zero or negative stars if I could. If you need a divorce, this is not the law from you should hire unless you want the process drawn out, a lawyer not to fight for you, documents to get lost, and to experience a high turnover in lawyers and paralegals. When I first met with Tristan (Lawyer #1) he advised me I would have to wait a little over a month before he could start handling my case. I was ok with that. I signed paperwork, paid my retainer and waited 2 months. I sent an email following up and was told my case was actually marked as "Closed". They re-opened it and I had to wait a couple more weeks before he could fit me in. I was advised that we could pass up my Financial Affidavit because they thought an agreement could be made. It couldn't but I was not advised otherwise until months later. I compiled my financial affidavit and sent it off. Waited and asked for an update. I believe I sent 3 emails over the course of 1 month to Tristan and Helen, his paralegal. I never heard anything from them. I had to contact my Ex- to ask his lawyer if they got my financial affidavit. It was never sent, and in fact Tristan hadn't even looked at it. To Tristan's credit, he did apologize and refund me some retainer money. He told me to expect a response within 24 business hours. Tristan wound up moving to a different state and passed my case along to a different lawyer in the office, Jilmala (Lawyer #2). Jilmala would take weeks to reply to me. I routine had to follow up on action items and then follow up on my follow up. During all of this I went through like 9 different paralegals and interns - Helen, Kelsi, Sandra, Shannon, Heather, Ashley, Caroline, Samantha, and Stephanie. I would sign documents in DocuSign with one of them and it would be lost. Id then be contacted weeks later asking me to sign a document I'd already sign but they had lost. The marital house was sold in the midst of the divorce, we had legal paperwork on how the funds would be handled. Month later when Jilmala was typing up the final MSA, she included how the house would be listed, sold, and funds dispersed. I had to tell her and send her documents of my house closing that they should have already had on file. It was an extremely frustrating experience and it escalated what could have been a more friendly divorce into a nit-picking and feisty one because of how long it dragged out. I did not feel like I had any advocates on my side. I was not counseled in any direction just asked what offer I wanted to make? or if an offer from the other lawyer was one I would want to accept. They basically were just an expensive vehicle to file all the legal paperwork for my divorce. No lawyers fought for me. Photos are of my emails with the various lawyers and paralegals; please note dates on the email threads. I would identify them as a Circus Ring and not a professional Law Firm. The only good thing that came out of this was I did eventually get a Cert of Marriage Dissolution; however that was originally sent with incorrect information. They had my Ex's state of birth and current county incorrect.

If I could give Chris more than 5 stars, I would! He has always gotten right back to me and truly has my back when I need him. I appreciate having an attorney who is reliable, responsive, and genuinely cares about his clients. Highly recommend!

I didn’t end up using this attorney, but I had such an important experience that I felt it was important to share with others. Several years ago I called this office for a consultation and once I shared that there was an element of physical violence involved, they made sure to get me in immediately. I felt safe, supported, and heard. I was given an honest and blunt opinion about my circumstance. I listened to it and ran with what I was told. Years later, I have 100% custody of my child and although this firm didn’t represent me in court, I strongly believe the one consultation meeting we had opened my eyes to the reality of my situation and gave me the strength to do what I needed to do.  Thank you for being honest with me and supporting me more than you’ll ever know. Thank you for telling me what had happened to me wasn’t okay and making me a priority when other firms made me wait days or weeks to be seen.

Absolute clown show of an attorney's office. If I could give zero stars I would. No one answers the phone, never return emails nor voicemails, extensive periods of time with no communication, etc. But...you can be damn sure they send you an invoice on time. Once you walk inside you know, just poorly run and vastly understaffed. Set my divorce back probably a year. Don't use.
-Matt

I would have left a review 12 years ago but don't think he had a Google page then. This guy is garbage and barely works for you. His excuse was he was dealing with some family issues. From some of the recent reviews doesn't look like he has done any better. If you are fighting a divorce or family issue find someone better. This is the worst option you can pick.

Gene represented me when I was going through a divorce and child custody battle for a few months before I requested a letter of relinquishment be written. I echo all the 1 and 2 star reviews. Waste of money and uses outdated practices for the state of IL. Gene wanted me to start paying child support to my ex when we didn’t even know where she was working or how much she was making at the time but advised me to start paying child support as “good faith” to potentially make me look better in court. I’m in a wheelchair and a father of 2 boys going through a custody battle and Gene props his feet on the table leans back in chair and tells me not to take offense to this but it seems like my dad was making all my decisions. He had me fill out a outdated financial affidavit from 2016. The law firm owes me a refund from my retainer and still have not received it. Never received an itemized invoice for the months he represented me. I could go on and on and I’ve been waiting a year and a half to write this review. Take it from me and go elsewhere.

Gene is hands down a go getter attorney. He is professional, well educated and knows his stuff. I’ve unfortunately been going through family law issues for longer than I’d like and he’s definitely been top notch and I highly recommend him. He will fight for you and your family.

My son hired Gene to help him with an X girl friend ( He had 2 girl's with )  So Gene went above and beyond to do nothing . For 2 years we never went to court to get anything done . So he has a nice bill that Gene should pay back . It's so sad that a dad just wanted things better with him and his girls and received nothing  Run Away
Everyone in the office is personable, down to earth, and soooo helpful! When you don't know what you need to do, but you need to do something, go to Gene!

Gene, Tammy, Dionna, Joan, and Deena were all amazing to work with.  I appreciate that Gene doesn’t over-bill.  Many lawyers will charge you an arm and a leg for little to no work because they will exaggerate work done.  I never felt like the bills I got were not fair for the work that was done and that’s all you can ask for from a law firm.  Tammy, Dionna, Joan and Deena do a fantastic job of helping with any questions and were always very professional.

Gene and his staff are wonderful. They are honest, transparent, knowledgeable, and communicative. I came to them with a difficult issue and they were extremely supportive and helpful in every way possible. I am so happy I was connected with this law firm and plan to continue to work with them in the future. I couldn't recommend them more!
Gene handled my divorce well and I trust his judgment when I have questions. Highly recommend!
Great people!!!

I saw Gene for a consult and found him to be honest, transparent, knowledgeable, and very helpful. He listened well, confirmed my course of action (though it meant lost business for himself) and did so without charging me a consultation fee. I was impressed with office staff warmth  and friendliness, too.
Just like most of the Bloomington/Normal Attorneys tell you that they can help you but they don't save your money not worth your time.
Very dedicated and friendly attorney and staff.   Does great work and goes above and beyond for clients.
Brucker Law went above and beyond for us!  Gene is very knowledgeable and his team worked very hard to help us. I highly recommend him!

This attorney does not believe in men's rights, he talks a good game up front but when it comes down to it he will either tell you its best to settle right before the hearing because he cant win after talking all the bs he talks. He couldn't discredit a GAL or he is to chicken to go up against a female attorney. He acts as if he's fighting for you and what you feel is in the best interest of your children but truly he's so the other side the entire time whispering his strategy to the opposing counsel. He could see clear violations in GALs report but stated it looks bad to fight with a GAL regarding her report on the stand. HELLO ISN"T THIS YOUR JOB???? Gene needs to get some guts or get out of the business. We've already had issues with the newest court order and his ex following and he answered the first email but now all of a sudden he's MIA, but the tracking on emails shows he's gotten every email and opened them and choose not to respond nor to any of the numerous phone calls he's received. Guess this is the norm for him as other posts say the same thing. Do NOT hire this man especially if you are a VET cause he could care less if you kept his butt safe for all those years you served. Today this day a year after our hearing we still have not received a single breakdown of his charges what he worked on what we were charged for. Absolutely NOTHING. ILARDC complaint coming next. Your just another paycheck to him on the backs of your kids stay clear of this attorney.
Less
This review is from a person who hired this attorney.
Gene is by far the best lawyer I've ever hired

Huge thanks to Mr. Arkell and the Dunn Law firm team including Brynn, Kayla and colleagues that helped my husband and me with our Green Card application. They were outstanding every step of the way, from helping us understand the requirements, compile the documents to make a compelling case, fill out all the paperwork, and navigate the different stages of the process. The process was stressful, but we always felt well supported and the Dunn Law team was super knowledgeable, highly experienced, and very attentive and thoughtful. Many thanks and highly recommend this firm.

I had an excellent experience with Dunn Law Firm for my green card process. From start to finish, the entire journey —from I-140 to Adjustment of Status (AOS), was completed in about 9 months, and my green card was approved just 48 hours after the interview.

Jim Arkell, my lead attorney, was exceptional throughout. He guided me with clarity, helped me make important decisions with confidence, and always provided timely, thoughtful answers. My AOS application was prepared and filed in just 3–4 days, and any questions I had were answered within 24 hours or less.
Kayla and Brynn- You are awesome :)

The entire Dunn Law team was highly professional, organized, and responsive at every step. I always felt supported and well-informed. If you're looking for a firm that combines deep immigration expertise with excellent client care, I highly recommend them.

My review is long overdue, but I am extremely grateful to Mr. Arkell and his team (Brittany Joyner and Kayla Clark)who worked on my petition. It was a pleasure working with them, and I appreciate their answers to my questions. I highly recommend Dunn Law firm for EB petitions.
I had great experience with this law firm. They are very responsive and try to make everything streamlined and as smooth as it gets.

Very grateful for the professional support with my successful NIW petition and adjustment of status application. Jim Arkell and his team were always available to answer all my questions, and answered even non-urgent questions within 24 hours, always consulting with a knowledgeable attorney as needed. The NIW petition was prepared professionally and with exceptional understanding. I strongly recommend Dunn Law Firm to anyone seeking immigration law assistance or representation.

I am delighted with Arkell's services, They answered my questions very quickly and they wrote a perfect petition for me,I strongly recommend them to anyone who wants to apply for US permanent residency through NIW.

This review is long due since my mother received her green card back in summer. I am reflecting on great things that happened in 2024 and this was one of them. I enjoyed working with the staff at this firm. Beenish and Brynn were always very responsive and responsible. My emails and questions were replied very quickly with care. Everything went smoothly in the process, and I have been referring my friends to them since then.

I had an outstanding experience collaborating with their team on my immigration petition. Their professionalism, attention to detail, and dedication to ensuring every aspect of the process was handled efficiently made the entire journey smooth and stress-free. They were incredibly supportive, promptly addressing all my questions and providing valuable guidance every step of the way. Their expertise and commitment truly exceeded my expectations, and I couldn’t have asked for a better team to assist me.

We are very satisfied with our experience during all AOS processes with the Dunn Law Firm. They were very professional and always replied our questions quickly.
We will recommend for colleagues and friends their work, as we have a great and successful experience!!!

I highly recommend Dr. Jim Arkell and his Team to anyone seeking advice for permanent resident application. I really appreciate the professionalism, dedication, and exceptional communication of the Team, always available for any inquires and providing regularly updates.

I am incredibly grateful to Jim Arkell Immigration Service for their outstanding support with my NIW I-140 petition. Their expertise, professionalism, and dedication made the entire process seamless and stress-free. Jim and his team were always available to answer my questions, guide me through every step, and ensure my petition was strong and well-prepared. Thanks to their excellent service, my I-140 was successfully approved. I highly recommend them to anyone seeking reliable and top-notch immigration services!
Dunn Law very professional, and responsive to any inquires. Provide honest consultation. you are in save hands with them

Exceptional, professionalism and experienced. They successfully filed my petition with precision, efficiency and great attention to detail. Throughout the process, they communicated clearly, answered my questions, and demonstrated a deep understanding of legal procedures.

I would give more stars if I could. This is an amazing team. Dunn Law did a great job with my I-140 petition. Jim Arkell was friendly and helpful, and the team was always quick to respond. He accepted my case when many other immigration attorneys turned it down.They made the process smooth, and my case got approved in about ten months. I've already recommended them to friends, and everyone’s been happy!
Awesome support and guidance..!

Highly Recommend for NIW Services: Senior Software Engineer

I had an excellent experience working with Jim Arkell on my National Interest Waiver (NIW) case. He is extremely knowledgeable, detail-oriented, and truly committed to clients' success. From start to finish, Jim Arkell made the complex process clear and manageable, providing expert guidance at every step.

Thanks to his professionalism and dedication, my petition was approved, and I couldn’t be happier. I highly recommend Jim Arkell to anyone seeking reliable, top-notch immigration services.

Yes, my green card was approved and Dunn law has been with me all the way through. I went down the EB-2 journey, and since I initially filed (for I-140) all the way until I received physical green card in my mailbox - only a year and a half has passed. I am more than grateful to Jim and others from his team - Thomas, Kayla, Brittany and Brynn for being there all the way and for answering every question I had very quickly. I felt like I had a lot of support and people who I can trust and rely on. I would recommend Jim's law firm to everyone.

I worked with these brilliant and professional people and at the end I got everything planned for. To be short, I strongly recommend this group of people for your immigration process because, as I mentioned earlier, they are kind, professional and caring. Thanks!

I am delighted with the services Mr. Arkell and his team rendered to me when I applied for the national interest waiver. They were beneficial in collating the required documents from the start until the time of filing. The process was very smooth with no hindrance. Ultimately, my NIW recently got approved, and I am on my way to becoming a permanent resident of the United States, all thanks to Mr. Arkell and his team.

I am thrilled to share that my NIW EB2 petition was recently approved, thanks to the exceptional support from Jim Arkell and his legal team at Dunn Law Firm. My case required detailed and insightful explanations to clearly convey the significance of my work. Jim and his team took the time to thoroughly understand my work and credentials, comprehensively presenting my case. Jim’s extensive academic and university experience gave him a unique edge in evaluating my case on its merits. The team’s deep understanding was consistently reassuring throughout the petition process. I would like to extend special thanks to Kayla Clark for her crucial role in promptly submitting the petition and supporting documents and to Brittany Joyner for her invaluable assistance.  I highly recommend Jim Arkell and his team at Dunn Law Firm to anyone pursuing an EB1 or NIW EB2-based green card. Thank you, Jim and team, for your outstanding support and dedication.

Jim Arkell and his legal team helped me with my employment-based EB1A and I-485 petitions that were approved recently. My case needed careful explanations to convey my work's impact accurately. Jim showed great care in understanding my work and credentials in the proper light and presented it comprehensively. We received an RFE after the initial submission, to which Jim guided me to parse and lay more objective evidence persuasive enough to win our case. Jim and his team then carefully prepared the rebuttal, resulting in our approval.
Jim is easy to work with and his academic and university experience gives him an edge in understanding each case based on its merits. His gentle demeanor and his understanding of my case were always reassuring. Kayla Clark was instrumental in helping me throughout and promptly submitting the petition and supporting documents. Brittany Joyner also helped me in the process. Previously, I also had a successful NIW petition filed by Jim and team that gave us an earlier priority date. I highly recommend Jim Arkell and his team at Dunn Law Firm to anyone looking for EB1A or EB2 based green card process. Thank you again Arkell and team for the huge help!

Just need to fill the gaps(your expertise,information, research etc.). I am very satisfied with their service. My I140 appoved in 5 months.

Highly recommend this firm. They filed EB1 and EB2 cases for me, and both cases got approved. Once I hired them, they only took 2 weeks to prepare all the material and file my cases (and this was during the new year's holidays). They are fast, professional, and better than any firm that I've heard of.

I was fortunate to know and work with Dunn Law Firm for my NIW green card application. All people in the team are extremely professional in supporting me preparing the documents, and I am always impressed with the way they take care the customers. Although the entire process was never shortened in the Immigration Office, I was happy that we had no issues or additional documents required after the submission till the day receiving the approval notice.

I am thrilled to share my experience with Jim Arkell, his team, and Dunn Law Firm. Despite the challenging times we are all facing, they made the process of obtaining my green card fast and smooth. From the initial consultation to the final approval, their team was professional, knowledgeable, and always responsive to my questions and concerns. Their expertise and dedication ensured that everything went smoothly, and I felt supported every step of the way. I highly recommend Jim Arkell, his team, and Dunn Law Firm to anyone seeking immigration assistance. Thank you for helping me achieve this important milestone!

They also have kind staff who are always available to attend to any questions you may have. If you are looking for credible experts for your immigration needs especially NIW, look no further than Attorney Jim and Dunn Law.

Jim Arkell and his team were instrumental in my successful EB-2 NIW visa application. Their expert guidance and unwavering support made a significant difference. They were always responsive to my questions and provided thorough assistance throughout the process. Thanks to their excellent work, my green card was approved without an interview. I highly recommend their services to anyone seeking professional and reliable immigration assistance.

Jim, Kayla and the team were fantastic helping us through I-140 and AOS petitions for an EB-2 NIW. They were thorough, insightful and their expertise showed in the advise they gave. I could not recommend them more!

Mr. Arkell, Kayla and the team are very helpful, knowledgeable, and highly professional. I had a great experience working with their team on my immigration petition. They are very efficient and honest. They are really really good on what they do. I highly recommend them (10 out of 10).

I had a great experience working with Jim and Kayla on my NIW application. They are very professional, responsive, and knowledgeable, overall the best team you could have to guide you through this process. I am deeply grateful for their help and guidance which made this process smooth and efficient, and I would highly recommend working with them.
Very experienced and supportive team. I recommend 100%. Whole process of filing petition is very easy because they will explain each and every step with clarity. Thank you Dunn Law.

I am incredibly grateful to Mr. Jim Arkell and his team at Dunn Law Firm for their outstanding support and guidance throughout my immigration journey. Their expertise and knowledge in immigration law are truly commendable. They first helped me secure approval in the EB2-NIW category and later filed for my I-140 in the EB1A category, both of which were successfully approved. I just got approval on my I-485 as well. Their deep understanding of the complexities involved in these cases was evident every step of the way.

In addition to their expertise, Mr. Jim Arkell and his team were always responsive and supportive. They promptly answered all my questions and kept me informed throughout the entire process, significantly reducing my stress and uncertainty. There were instances where they were working over weekends in order to file my petition on time. Furthermore, their services were very affordable, offering competitive rates without compromising on quality. Their transparency regarding fees and the value they provided made a significant positive impact on my experience.

I wholeheartedly recommend Mr. Jim Arkell and Dunn Law Firm to anyone seeking immigration legal services. Their professionalism, dedication, and client-centered approach have been invaluable in helping me achieve my dream of obtaining a green card. Thank you, Jim Arkell, Kayla Clark and Brittany Martin, for your excellent service and for making this important milestone possible.

I hired Dunn Law Firm help me in my immigration case. Mr. Arkell and his team did a great job. They are very professional, honest, reliable, and efficient. I highly recommend Dunn Law firm without any hesitation.

I worked on my I-140 case with Mr. Jim Arkell and his team, who offered exceptional support throughout every phase. They were always ready to assist with any inquiries I had during the process, including after we received the referral letters. Upon addressing their questions, they promptly and skillfully filed the I-140 petition. My case was approved in 121 days, and I am deeply grateful to Mr. Arkell and his team for their guidance and support.

"The lawyers of this firm are extremely proficient, genuine, and truthful. They put in a lot of effort to represent you until you achieve a favorable outcome. They are exceptionally prompt and knowledgeable. My family and I feel extremely privileged to have taken their services to attain permanent residency in the United States. I offer my heartfelt gratitude to Tom Arkell and Stacy Rinkenberger for their excellent work."

Mr Jim Arkell and his brother, Mr Thomas Arkell, are very knowledgeable attorneys and behaved very professionally throughout the process. It started with an email consultation, followed by an evaluation of my CV and eligibility for NIW. I then followed up with a telephone call to help me understand how to position my case and whom to approach for letters of support. Their guidance and clarity of the stage of the case was also thorough. The firm staff are also very commendable; Kayla and Brittany were always quick to respond to my queries. I was presented with the case cover letter, and I was very satisfied with its content. The case was carefully researched, and the cover letter very well written. It came as no surprise that, only within two weeks of submission, the case was adjudicated favourably. I cannot recommend Mr Arkell and his firm enough.

I recently had the pleasure of working with Jim Arkell and his team at Dunn Law Firm, specifically Kayla and Brittany, for my National Interest Waiver self-petition. From the outset, they were nothing short of exceptional. They played a crucial role in revising my documents, crafting a compelling petition letter on my behalf, and meticulously answering my numerous questions, especially regarding the I-140 form.

My case was approved in just five days from my petition date. This level of effectiveness speaks volumes about the quality of service provided by Dunn Law Firm.

I cannot speak highly enough of Jim, Kayla, Brittany, and the entire team, and wholeheartedly recommend Dunn Law Firm to anyone seeking legal representation in immigration matters.

I had an outstanding experience with the Dunn Law Firm, especially with my lawyer, Jim Arkell, and his dedicated team. They played a crucial role in helping me secure my green card, and I couldn't be more grateful for their exceptional service.

From the beginning, Jim and his team exhibited a high level of professionalism and efficiency. They were always available to address my questions and concerns, providing timely and clear responses that helped alleviate any uncertainties I had during the process. The Dunn Law Firm demonstrated a remarkable ability to navigate the complexities of the immigration process swiftly without compromising the quality of their work. This efficiency made the entire experience smooth and stress-free for me.

I wholeheartedly recommend the Dunn Law Firm to anyone in need of immigration services. Jim Arkell and his team are not only experts in their field but also compassionate professionals who genuinely care about their clients' success. Thank you, Dunn Law Firm, for making a significant difference in my life.

Jim, and his team Kayla and Brittany, did an amazing job in helping me in getting a green card. I'm very grateful for their efforts, they literally changed my life for the better. I've compared many other law firms before but was never confident in choosing any of them, when I crossed paths with Jim Arkell, I had no doubts in choosing his firm and trusting them in this process, so I'd definitely recommend them 110%!

It was a great pleasure to work with Mr. Arkell's team. Though out all 10 years their support was invaluable and professional. From the very start till the great success at the end.

Thank you guys for the great work.

reviews.text: I would like to start out by stating that if you are checking these reviews, please pay close attention to the lowest ratings.  This law firm might be able to handle routine cases, but if it is complicated please steer clear.  In November 2021, my family hired this law firm to represent my son in a felony case.  I specifically chose this law firm because there were elements of the case that needed to be investigated further, and this law firm conveyed to me that they would do just that. Not only did they NOT investigate they also failed to properly advise my son of the consequences of taking a plea and how a sentencing hearing was conducted.  Initially, Ken Eckhauser was the attorney assigned to my son's case. He attended 2-3 hearings where during the last hearing via Zoom, he was admonished by the judge for not being properly dressed.  A few weeks after this hearing, Mark Johnson took over the case.  We were now into the month of March/April 2022.  My son contacted me from jail to say some other attorney came to visit him and he had no idea who it was.  Keep in mind, from November until this date I had no communication from the law firm but they had all of the $25,000 we had paid.  Mr. Johnson informed me after having met with the State Attorney's (SA) office that they would not budge on their offer of a 23-year sentence but that if my son plead to the most serious of the charges that we should go to a sentencing hearing where he believed that he could get a much lower sentence.  What we did not find out until the day of the sentencing hearing was that 1) it is a mini-trial overseen by the judge, and 2) the SA presented that they were now asking for a 28 year sentence.  At the end of the hearing, my son was sentenced to 20 years for a Class X - aggravated  battery/discharge of a firearm. If the sentence itself wasn't disappointing so was Mr. Johnson's performance.  It was like he did not know my son's case at all and did not press the witnesses as their testimony did not match that of what they told police when initially interviewed, amongst other issues that became evident were never investigated.  Today our family is deep in the appeal process and trying to reverse all the bad representation my son received.  I still have yet to receive an itemized billing of what my $25,000 went to.
reviews.ownerResponse: Monica: Although you are expressing frustration 4 yrs later, I recall that we successfully avoided an attempted murder charge and were able to obtain a sentence below the 28 years that the State had pursued. I recall aggressively cross examining the victim when he testified about his best friend shooting him in the face. Despite my arguments for a lessor sentence, the Judge ruled that shooting a defenseless person in the face at point blank range, combined with a record of serious prior criminal offenses, required a 20 yr sentence. We recently reopened the file to confirm whether you previously voiced any issues or requested an itemization and it does not appear that you did. Please call our office so I can assist you further. As I told you years ago, I wish your family the best in dealing with this difficult case.

reviews.text:
I never thought I'd need legal representation, and when the need presented itself I was unsure what to do. After consulting other firms, I happened to get a call back from the Johnson Law Group. I knew right away that I'd be choosing them.
Everybody I spoke with was amazing.
I had the pleasure of working with Holly and the founding partner Mark. From the first phone call to the last phone call, and the emails between, I felt like I was in good hands. I will always remember that first in person meeting with Mark. He was very thorough, he did not try to speak over me, and he asked questions that left me feeling human. That my words mattered to him. He took his time explaining the legal process to me.
I left the office feeling calm, that I was no longer navigating things on my own and in the dark. I knew that I had a group advocating for me, and that they would not give less than one hundred percent.

Though I did not get the chance to meet Holly, most of the email correspondence was through her. All of the good things I've said apply to her as well, and I wanted that to be recognized.
Although there were times I didn't hear from the firm for awhile, I knew this was because they were giving it their all. Not once did I feel forgotten about. For anybody reading this review, consider reaching out to them as soon as you can. Their service in representation was more than worth it for me, and I trust they'd advocate for you just as hard.

Mark, Holly, and everyone else who worked on my case. I know I've said thank you many times already, but I truly can't put into words how much I appreciate you all. Thank you for your kindness, your patience, and for all of the hard work. I am glad to have had you by my side.

Looked at the invoice, nothing but a bunch of "conferences" between my attorney and his supervisor. So for a total of  $3000.00 I got a peice of paper. Also got told that their assistant would do most of the work at a cheaper rate and my attorney would only step in when needed, also a lie. All thebwork done was charged under his rate, and no one could answer why they are so many conferences for a simple parenting plan update. I paid less than this for several apparences with my previous attorney. Would not recommend to anyone unless you like wasting thousands of dollars.

reviews.text: Works for opposing counsel not for their client.

I was very sad to hear that my lawyer Zoe Arthurson-McColl is leaving the firm. She made me feel comfortable during tough decisions, and walked me through my case very thoroughly, and she made sure I understood everything that was happening.

I am extremely happy for her, and I hope nothing but the best on her next life endeavors.

Thank you for all you have done for me Zoe!! I appreciate you.

**March 2024 update: Gather round everyone coz the word of the day is "babysitting!"

See this all started last year when a receptionist at the Springfield office misplaced some of the important documents I personally handed in that my attorney was waiting for in Bloomington. Few weeks later I get a call asking where they were, causing massive delays along with a lecture on how I should've followed up... that's when I nominated myself as babysitter and since they're going to side with their team members across the board, naturally the finger pointing and accusations began. So, being the heartfelt understanding person I am, I went back to the same receptionist in Springfield and basically had to tell her how to do her job as I watched her every step of the way and even had her call to confirm the receipt. Bless her heart it was a success! So happy for the new accomplishment.

November 2023 was my last hearing and the very last time I spoke to my attorney ending the conversation with a fake handshake and a big fat thx for nothing because that's exactly what he did after draining my credit card with the fees I mentioned below that will remain in default since I have no plans on ever paying a dime on. And ya wonder why interest rates are sky high. Sorry not sorry.

Another month goes by and I'm still seeing charges come through so I call in and ask what on earth are you charging me if this was over in November? They're response was that I had to make a request to actually close out the file, so while I had her on the line I went ahead and did just that... made a simple request to close out the file and asked when the remaining balance in the trust fund account was gonna be mailed to me, their response was 4-6 weeks.

Here we are 6 weeks goes by and I just decided to check up on things coz you know, I've been having to babysit full grown working adults these days....the lady on the phone gives me an actual date of when the check was mailed, I kindly help her with a simple math correction and explain how I should've seen the check by now so I get transferred to the department that handles that sorta thing resulting in a number of unreturned messages being left.

After bouncing around for days with no response, I finally was able to reach someone that expressed to me the file hasn't actually been closed out yet so I was transferred again and left another message. After still not hearing anything from anyone I decided to call everyday twice a day until someone at this elementary 3 ring circus JOKE of a company that supposedly has been in business for X amount of years can give me a straight answer.

I'll be updating my progress periodically so stay tuned!

*Original post: Guess how much a child support modification should cost fellas? ZILCH! Guess how much it ended up costing me...$13k! Allow me to explain this nonsense before you move forward...all you need is proof of income and I don't even think you really need that cuz guess who already knows how much you make before you walk in the courtroom? The state duh!

Interested in knowing why it cost so much? It's called unnecessary leg work! Phone calls, conversations, email, paperwork, verifications, correspondences, signatures, faxing, filing, research, meetings, preparing, planning, rehearsing, etc etc etc.

You know why I know this? The briefcase chalked full of ammo that my lawyer used to fuel my hopes for the hearing just sat there. The state already had everyone's income with them and made the modification based solely on those figures in less than 5 minutes when it took 2 unnecessary months of back and forth BS with my attorney.

One hell of a lucrative scam if you ask me. Don't even bother. All you gotta do is put in a modification request with the state and they handle the rest. You think they would share that little bit of info with me, nope. They took advantage of an opportunity cuz I didn't know any better and ROYALLY milked it.

Do not hire this law firm. I had a simple case to amend a Qdro that required a court date. They never attempted to get a court date and they charged every month for strategy meetings on how to move forward.
The client care department said my case should have been put on hold until the courts opened for hearings again , because of the pandemic.
The first attorney charged for acquiring documents that I had already provided and said I had a strong case. He was fired or quit after about 11 months and was replaced by another.
One of the partners of the firm said there wouldn’t be any charge for the second attorney to take over the case then I received a bill for over $2,000.00.
He proceeded to send out for the same documents the first attorney was already paid to acquire and I was charged a second time. Then he told me I didn’t have a case.
After all of this the second attorney quit so they wanted to assign a third attorney to me.
I told them I couldn’t afford to keep paying people to gather documents and never get to court.
Client care is a department that doesn’t do anything except agree with the client that the case was mishandled.  They don’t ever try to correct the problem.
They constantly say that I signed a contract , and yes I did , but , shouldn’t they have to uphold their end of the agreement ?
This firm charged over $11,000.00 and never did more than shuffle a few papers.
Try to find a smaller firm that might have one honest attorney working that will actually try to do what’s best for their clients instead of what is best for their wallets.
Good luck to you if you decide to hire this firm
James R Luczaj Sr.
